reasoning:
The Federal Reserve officially ended its Quantitative Tightening (QT) program in December 2025 and pivoted to a regime of Reserve Management Purchases (RMPs) to maintain ample liquidity. Since Jan 1, 2026, the balance sheet has expanded for 10 consecutive weeks without a single dip. 

Looking at recent data, standard weekly increases have grown to roughly +$15B (as seen in early February and early March). During the week encompassing the 15th of the month, Mortgage-Backed Securities (MBS) paydowns slightly offset these gains. However, this paydown effect is far outpaced by current purchase volumes: in closely analogous mid-month periods, the net balance sheet still grew safely (+$4.7B for the week of Jan 21, and +$8.0B for the week of Feb 18). Furthermore, March 2026 has the exact same calendar alignment as February 2026 (the 15th is a Sunday, meaning MBS paydowns hit the same number of days before the Wednesday tally). 

Because the structural purchases are substantially larger than the temporary MBS mid-month drag, the WSHOSHO series is almost guaranteed to print another positive week on March 18. Given the unblemished 10-week streak under the new mandate and perfectly mirroring historical mechanical dynamics, I assign a 94% probability to an increase.
